items.title: Cloud Cover: There’s More to Cloud AI Cost Than Token Price
items.description_en: Platform selection is not a single procurement decision but three linked choices—model, architecture, and control-plane—each with different implications for cost, lock-in, and governance. Leaders must not conflate these choices or optimize simply based on headline token prices, or they will systematically over-spend,…
summary: The decisions concerning model choice, workforce architecture, and enterprise platforms.
control plane help leadership connect workload economics to platform readiness so CXOs can make the trade-offs explicit.
Leaders must not conflate these choices or optimize simply based on headline token prices, or they will systematically over-spend, under-govern
items._summary_source: Platform selection is not a single procurement decision but three linked choices—model, architecture, and control-plane—each with different implications for cost, lock-in, and governance. Leaders must not conflate these choices or optimize simply based on headline token prices, or they will systematically over-spend, under-govern, or both. What used to be a procurement decision—negotiated on headline pricing and master-agreement leverage—is now a strategic platform decision that shapes innovation velocity, governance control, and cost economics at scale. The decisions concerning model choice, workforce architecture, and enterprise platforms. control plane help leadership connect workload economics to platform readiness so CXOs can make the trade-offs explicit.
items.chart_image_alt: Cloud Cover: There’s More to Cloud AI Cost Than Token Price

items.title: Why We Still Need a CIO in the AI-First Era
items.description_en: Governing how a company uses AI to build solutions, at what cost, and for what purpose demands a stronger CIO. Every workplace team can now build and deploy agents directly, yet without governance over what gets built and how AI spending is tied to outcomes. This causes organizations to confront more and more complexi…
summary: Governing how a company uses AI to build solutions, at what cost, and for what purpose demands a stronger CIO.
Every workplace team can now build and deploy agents directly
BCG가 짚은 핵심 쟁점과 영향 포인트를 함께 확인할 수 있습니다.
items._summary_source: Governing how a company uses AI to build solutions, at what cost, and for what purpose demands a stronger CIO. Every workplace team can now build and deploy agents directly, yet without governance over what gets built and how AI spending is tied to outcomes. This causes organizations to confront more and more complexity. The traditional CIO mandate to control scarce IT delivery is collapsing, just as ungoverned AI—fragmented agents, runaway costs, brittle architectures—becomes an enterprise-scale risk. CIOs who pivot from service provider to orchestrator— owning enterprise intelligence, digital labor, and value pathways—will define which companies turn AI abundance into competitive advantage.
items.chart_image_alt: Why We Still Need a CIO in the AI-First Era

items.title: Banking Modernization at Scale: A Conversation with Patrick Wright
items.description_en: As National Australia Bank’s group executive of technology and enterprise operations, Patrick Wright led one of the most ambitious tech transformations in Australian banking. Now he sits down with Bain’s Damian Stephenson to explain how he pulled it off and what he learned.

items.title: Trauma's toll on the workforce
